question_text: Explain why we always see the same side of the Moon from Earth.
correct_answer: The Moon is tidally locked to Earth. It rotates on its axis at the same rate as it orbits Earth, so the same side always faces us.
explanation: The Moon takes about 27 days to orbit Earth and also takes about 27 days to rotate once on its axis. This means the rotation and orbit are synchronised, a process called tidal locking caused by Earth's gravity.
